title: Court orders appointment of special administrators for Euro Exchange Securities UK Limited
summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority has appointed special administrators for Euro Exchange Securities UK Limited, effectively ceasing the firm's trading activities, and the company will work to return client money as quickly as possible. This regulatory update is relevant to firms operating in the UK financial sector, particularly those involved in securities trading, as it highlights the FCA's actions against non-compliant entities.
action_required: Firms should review their compliance procedures to ensure adherence to FCA regulations and consider the potential implications of special administration on their own operations.

title: Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited enters administration
summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority has announced that Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited, a firm authorised by the FCA, has entered administration, which may have implications for consumers and businesses that interact with the firm. This update is primarily informational, and compliance officers should be aware of the development to assess any potential impact on their organisation.
action_required: Compliance officers should review the firm's relationships with Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited and its trading names to determine if any adjustments are needed to ensure ongoing compliance with regulatory requirements.

title: FCA decides to fine Carlos Ricardo Fuenmayor £99,600 for disclosure failures
summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority has fined Carlos Ricardo Fuenmayor £99,600 for disclosure failures, highlighting the importance of transparency in financial dealings. This regulatory update serves as a reminder for companies to ensure compliance with disclosure requirements to avoid similar penalties.
action_required: Review and verify current disclosure procedures to ensure compliance with FCA regulations.

title: Opening the door to mortgages: rules focused on better outcomes for people
summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority has proposed changes to mortgage rules to improve access for first-time buyers, older borrowers, and the self-employed, aiming to provide more flexibility in mortgage lending. These changes are intended to reflect the evolving nature of work and income, allowing lenders to adapt their mortgage products and assessment processes accordingly.
action_required: Review and assess current mortgage lending practices and policies to determine necessary adjustments in line with the proposed regulatory changes.

title: FCA proposes changes to help more people access mortgages
summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority has proposed changes to mortgage rules to increase accessibility for first-time buyers, older borrowers, and the self-employed, while maintaining strong consumer protections. These changes aim to give lenders more flexibility in considering individual circumstances and developing products that better meet people's needs.
action_required: Compliance teams should review the proposed mortgage rule changes and assess their potential impact on current lending practices and procedures.

title: BIS Quarterly Review, June 2026
summary: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) has released a quarterly review highlighting the need for central banks to recalibrate their lending operations to enhance market resilience, which may impact banking institutions' liquidity management and risk mitigation strategies. This update requires attention from relevant departments to assess and potentially adjust their processes to align with the BIS's recommendations.
action_required: Banking institutions should review their liquidity management and risk mitigation processes to identify areas for improvement in line with the BIS's suggestions.

title: Credit supply in the wake of distressed bank acquisitions
summary: The Bank for International Settlements has released a paper examining the credit supply effects of sale-of-business bank resolutions, providing insights into how an acquiring bank reshapes credit allocation after a resolution. This research may inform compliance strategies and risk management practices for banks involved in distressed acquisitions, potentially leading to moderate process adjustments.
action_required: Review the paper's findings and assess their implications for the bank's credit supply and risk management processes to determine necessary adjustments.

title: The anatomy of stablecoin transactions
summary: The Bank for International Settlements has released a report on the anatomy of stablecoin transactions, highlighting the complexity of these transactions and their potential impact on regulatory interpretation. This report provides valuable insights for compliance officers to reassess their understanding of stablecoin activity and its implications for regulatory compliance.
action_required: Compliance officers should review the report and assess the potential impact on existing regulatory frameworks and processes within the next 90 days.

title: Strengthening the going-concern role of AT1: options and trade-offs
summary: The Bank for International Settlements has issued a regulatory update regarding the strengthening of Additional Tier 1 instruments as going-concern capital, highlighting the need for improvements in their effectiveness. This update may require adjustments to banks' capital management and resolution planning processes to ensure compliance with the proposed enhancements.
action_required: Banks should review their AT1 instruments and recapitalisation strategies to ensure alignment with the BIS's proposed enhancements and assess potential operational changes needed to support an orderly resolution.

title: Asset price bubbles and systemic risk in money market funds
summary: The Bank for International Settlements has released a report analyzing the systemic risk contribution of money market funds, highlighting the influence of fund characteristics on systemic risk during asset price bubbles. This report provides valuable insights for financial institutions to reassess their risk management strategies and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
action_required: Review and update risk management policies to account for the potential systemic risk contribution of money market funds, as outlined in the BIS report.
